//: DIRT-1 'The On-Ramp' (docs/design/criminal_underworld_content_arc.md §5), Sonnet 5. First batch of the criminal-underworld content arc: a gambling-debt spiral that converges into the SHIPPED compromised_syndicate -> syndicate_shakedown cascade (core_events.json), giving that cascade a second, content-authored on-ramp alongside back_alley_bribe. All 11 events are scope:'avatar' per the authoring contract §4.3 (sidesteps the scope:any weight-blast-radius and NULL-team tier-leak hazards for free). Flag lifecycle (owes_the_book -> dodging_debt/running_errands -> in_the_life, converging on compromised_syndicate) follows §5.1's table exactly; no orphaned flags, no dead-end set_flags (in_the_life is a disclosed DIRT-2 forward hook but is already read three times within this batch). detection_risk is never read or written anywhere in this file -- collision #1 in §2: DIRT-* tracks legal jeopardy with boolean flags only (owes_the_book/dodging_debt/running_errands/been_lifting/in_the_life), never the PED meter. The syndicate stays faceless on 'unknown' (dirt_debt_sold); the one new NAMED associate is the_bookie ('Sal'), added to contacts.json in this change (role:friend, zero registry code touch). Cooldown additions beyond the design doc's explicit numbers (the doc left these unspecified for several slots): dirt_the_marker (14d -- 'work it off'/'duck the call' both leave owes_the_book active, so the marker gate stays satisfied and needs pacing), dirt_lookout (30d -- none of its choices clear its gate), dirt_busted (30d -- 'fight it' keeps been_lifting active), dirt_family_finds_out (40d -- by design 'a promise is not an exit', nothing clears in_the_life), dirt_street_rep (40d -- same), dirt_clean_break (30d -- 'one last score' deliberately keeps in_the_life so the pull-back-in can recur, per the doc's own note). Every other event either clears its own gate on at least one branch or was given an explicit cooldown/min_days_since by the doc; check_event_graph_integrity #4 (no unpaced loops) holds throughout. REVIEW FIX (Fable 5, doc S5.3): dirt_sticky_fingers additionally gates on hs_started+14d (dirt_lookout's belt-and-braces idiom) -- the Destitute backstory tier starts at $50, so its funds<200 gate alone COULD hold on day 2 of a fresh save, and since this file sorts alphabetically before hs_onboarding_events.json the dispatcher's first-satisfied-in-file-order sweep would give it a weight-0.08 roll at stealing the avatar's day-2 slot from the weight-1.0 hs_first_day. The anchor flag costs nothing at any later tier (hs_started stays active for the life of every avatar who passed through HS); the one disclosed dead branch is a hypothetical future avatar who never fired hs_first_day. dirt_hot_merch's optional bad_product engine-flag tie-in (doc §5.2 item 9, 'author's call, disclose if used') was NOT used -- kept as pure fencing-flavor texture, no HustleService coupling in this batch. dirt_busted's contact is 'unknown' (booking is faceless, mirroring roster_availability_events.json's narcotics_arrest) rather than agent_diaz, since this beat is tier-agnostic petty crime, not the pro-era legal-jeopardy triad. Weights: highest is dirt_debt_sold at 0.5 (avatar scope, single subject -- the ×136-subject blast-radius concern in check #6 doesn't apply), everything else <=0.30, matching §5.1's ceiling. || DIRT-2 'The Fix' (docs/design/criminal_underworld_dirt2_the_fix.md, Opus 4.8 spec; Sonnet 5 build), appended to this same file per §5's author's-call (arc stays one document). 11 new events, all scope:'avatar', every one additionally gated 'field tier >= 2' (§4 item 3 -- immune to the NULL-team -1 sentinel, and structurally inert during the tier-0 HS onboarding phase in playtest, so no day-1/2 slot-theft hazard like DIRT-1 had). Two ramps converge on the same fix cascade: dirt_the_ask (flag_active compromised_syndicate, the primary 'they own you' ramp) and dirt_made_useful (flag_active in_the_life + flag_inactive compromised_syndicate, the second ramp -- its let_them_in choice is a NEW third writer of compromised_syndicate, back-feeding the shipped +365 syndicate_shakedown one tier up, mirroring DIRT-1's own two-on-ramps-one-deep-end shape). Flag lifecycle exactly per spec §5.1: on_the_hook (set by dirt_the_ask/dirt_made_useful engage choices; read by dirt_the_tip, dirt_shave_runs, dirt_turn_informant_a, dirt_walk_away_clean; cleared by dirt_walk_away_clean, dirt_turn_informant_*, dirt_league_investigation's take_the_suspension), shaving_runs (set by dirt_shave_runs' shave/throw branches; read by dirt_teammate_knows, dirt_league_investigation, dirt_turn_informant_b; cleared by dirt_league_investigation's take_the_suspension and dirt_turn_informant_*), threw_a_game (set by dirt_shave_runs' throw branch only; read by dirt_permanent_ban; cleared by dirt_permanent_ban and dirt_league_investigation's take_the_suspension -- belt-and-braces per spec), banned_for_life (set by dirt_permanent_ban, NEVER cleared -- terminal by design per §4 item 4; read as a gate by dirt_league_investigation/dirt_permanent_ban/dirt_turn_informant_a+b (flag_inactive, so a banned player can't re-enter the cascade) AND as flag_active by dirt_blacklisted -- an in-batch reader, stronger than the disclosed forward-hook floor), cooperated_with_league (set by dirt_turn_informant_a+b's cooperate choice; disclosed DIRT-4 forward hook, no in-batch reader, legal per check #3). syndicate_marked gets two NEW writers (dirt_the_ask's tell_them_no, dirt_turn_informant_*'s cooperate -- informing IS defiance) feeding the shipped syndicate_enforcers @ +14d untouched. detection_risk is never touched anywhere in this batch (§2 collision #1 continued -- a fixer who never juiced must never draw caught_juicing). dirt_turn_informant is authored as TWO events (_a gates flag_active on_the_hook + flag_inactive shaving_runs; _b gates flag_active shaving_runs) per §5.2 item 9's explicit instruction -- prerequisites are OR-shaped so the framework's §3 rule (OR is two events, never a single leaf) requires the split; both share identical consequences on their 'cooperate'/'stay_in' choices. Cooldowns beyond the doc's explicit numbers: dirt_league_investigation (30d -- 'deny and stonewall' deliberately keeps shaving_runs active, mirroring DIRT-1's dirt_busted 'fight it' pacing precedent) and dirt_permanent_ban (30d belt-and-braces, though its single lawyer_it_down choice already self-blocks by clearing threw_a_game). No orphaned prerequisites: every flag read here is set somewhere in the whole Content folder (compromised_syndicate/in_the_life/syndicate_marked pre-exist from DIRT-1/core; on_the_hook/shaving_runs/threw_a_game/banned_for_life are new, all set within this same batch). Contacts: zero contacts.json touch (§6) -- agent_diaz carries every suspension/legal-jeopardy beat (mirroring roster_availability_events.json's suspended_* precedent verbatim, vs. 'unknown' for tier-agnostic petty crime), 'unknown' carries the faceless syndicate ask/tip/shave beats, coach_reyes/family_mom carry the two door-out epilogues. CareerManager reads zero Entity_Flags today (§2 collision #2, verified) -- banned_for_life's real teeth in THIS batch are the season-length suspension + the rival/league edge + dirt_blacklisted's epilogue, never a claimed career-ending mechanic. Zero Assets/Simulation/ touch by construction (§4 item 1) -- 'shave a run'/'throw a game' are pure consequence bundles plus a complicity flag, never an AtBatResolver/LeagueSimulator hook.

events.prompt: A card game in the back room of Sal's place has a seat open, and the buy-in is exactly the money you don't have.

choices.label: Cash out early
choices.outcome: You take your small win off the table before it turns into a story you have to explain later.

choices.label: Chase the pot
choices.outcome: You push everything back in. The cards don't cooperate, and the number you owe by the end of the night is not a number you have.

choices.label: Don't sit down
choices.outcome: You watch one hand from the doorway and decide your rent doesn't need this kind of help.

events.prompt: Sal finds you outside practice like he already knew your schedule. 'You know why I'm here.'

choices.label: Pay the marker
choices.outcome: You hand over the principal and the vig without a word. Sal counts it twice and nods once.

choices.label: Work it off
choices.outcome: Sal says he's got 'something lighter' if you're short on cash but not on time. You don't love how easy he makes it sound.

choices.label: Duck the call
choices.outcome: You let it ring. Whatever this buys you, it isn't peace of mind.

events.prompt: The number that calls isn't Sal's. Whoever bought your marker doesn't do small talk, and they already know where you practice.

choices.label: Accept their terms
choices.outcome: There's no negotiating a number this size with people like this -- only agreeing to whatever comes next.

choices.label: Try to disappear
choices.outcome: You change your number. It buys you exactly four days before a different number finds you anyway.

events.prompt: Sal's 'something lighter' turns out to be a gym bag and an address. 'Don't open it. Don't be late.'

choices.label: Make the clean run
choices.outcome: You drop the bag, take the envelope, and drive home under the speed limit the whole way.

choices.label: Skim a little off the top
choices.outcome: You pocket a little extra before the drop and hope nobody's counting as closely as you are.

choices.label: Get cold feet, stall the drop
choices.outcome: You sit in the car for twenty minutes before you finally go through with it, and Sal notices you're late.

events.prompt: Marcus wants a lookout for something he won't fully explain, and he's offering actual cash for standing on a corner.

choices.label: Stand lookout
choices.outcome: Nothing happens, which is the whole job. Marcus pays you anyway, like it's a bigger deal than it looked.

choices.label: Walk home instead
choices.outcome: You tell him you're good and head home. Marcus shrugs like he expected that.

events.prompt: The gas station's cameras are pointed at the register, not the aisle you're standing in, and your wallet is doing that thing where it's basically empty.

choices.label: Grab it and go
choices.outcome: It's in your pocket and you're out the door before you've really decided to do it.

choices.label: Put it back
choices.outcome: You set it back on the shelf and buy nothing. Small thing. Feels bigger than it should.

events.prompt: A hand closes on your shoulder before you're two steps out the door. Loss prevention, and they've been watching longer than you thought.

choices.label: Take the plea
choices.outcome: A lawyer you can barely afford gets it down to a fine and a slap on the wrist. It still costs you two days you don't get back.

choices.label: Fight it
choices.outcome: You fight the charge on principle. It costs more, takes longer, and the outcome is about the same.

events.prompt: Mom sets a stack of unexplained cash down on the kitchen table without a word, and the silence after is worse than any question would be.

choices.label: Promise you'll stop
choices.outcome: You promise. She wants to believe you, and for a night, so do you.

choices.label: Lie about where it came from
choices.outcome: You make something up that almost holds together. She doesn't push. That's its own kind of answer.

choices.label: Tell her the truth
choices.outcome: You tell her everything. It's a hard conversation, and somehow an honest one is still better than the lie would've been.

events.prompt: A guy from the old crew has a car trunk full of things that fell off a truck, and he's offering a cut just to make a few calls.

choices.label: Make the calls, move it
choices.outcome: You make a few calls, everything moves, and the cut lands in your pocket by the weekend.

choices.label: Tell him it's not your problem
choices.outcome: You tell him you're out on this one. He doesn't argue, just moves on to someone who won't say no.

events.prompt: People at the field talk different around you now -- half respect, half distance, like the dirt on you finally shows.

choices.label: Lean into the reputation
choices.outcome: You let the reputation do some of the talking for you. People stop testing you as often.

choices.label: Keep a low profile
choices.outcome: You go out of your way to seem like exactly what you used to be. Fewer people buy it than you'd like.

events.prompt: Coach Reyes pulls you aside after everyone else has cleared the clubhouse. 'Whatever you've got going on outside this building -- it ends, or you do. Your call.'

choices.label: Get out clean
choices.outcome: You make the calls that end it -- all of it -- and brace for a cost that never quite comes due the way you expected.

choices.label: Just one last score
choices.outcome: You tell yourself this is the last one. You've told yourself that before.

events.prompt: The voice on the phone doesn't introduce itself. It doesn't have to. 'You made the show. That marker we're still holding just got a lot more interesting to us.'

choices.label: Hear them out
choices.outcome: You don't say yes to anything, not out loud. But you don't hang up either, and they both know what that means.

choices.label: Tell them no
choices.outcome: You tell them you're done being useful. The silence on the other end lasts a beat too long before the line goes dead.

choices.label: Stall, ask for time
choices.outcome: You say you need to think. They say take your time. Neither of you means it the friendly way.

events.prompt: Sal's old crew found out you made the show, and somebody up the chain made a call about you. 'They just want to talk. You're worth more to them now than you ever were owing money.'

choices.label: Let them in
choices.outcome: You take the meeting. Nobody makes you sign anything. You still walk out owned in a way you weren't walking in.

choices.label: Stay clean, they don't own you yet
choices.outcome: You tell them you're flattered but you're out. It's an easy no to give, this early -- they don't have anything on you yet.

events.prompt: They don't want a fix yet. They want a lineup, an injury report, anything that isn't public until it is. 'Just information. Nobody gets hurt over information.'

choices.label: Pass along the tip
choices.outcome: You tell them what you know. It's small, it's easy to justify, and the envelope that shows up after is bigger than the information deserved.

choices.label: Give them nothing
choices.outcome: You go quiet on the call. They don't push -- this time.

events.prompt: Information isn't what they're calling about this time. 'A run here, a run there. Nobody watching box scores that close will ever know the difference. We will.'

choices.label: Shave a run
choices.outcome: You give up a run in a spot nobody will ever ask you about. The money that lands after is real money for the first time.

choices.label: Throw the whole game
choices.outcome: You don't shave anything. You bury it, start to finish, and there's no version of tonight's box score that doesn't look like exactly what it was.

choices.label: Not this time
choices.outcome: You tell them you're not doing that. They don't argue. They also don't forget you're still on the hook.

events.prompt: Someone in the clubhouse noticed the effort dip in that one inning. Maybe it's nothing. Maybe he's in on it too.

choices.label: Bring him in, buy his silence
choices.outcome: You make it worth his while to have never seen anything. Now there are two of you carrying this instead of one.

choices.label: Deny it to his face
choices.outcome: You tell him he's imagining things. He doesn't push it, but he doesn't quite believe you either.

events.prompt: League security wants an interview, and your lawyer says the word 'interview' the way people say 'formality' when it isn't one.

choices.label: Take the suspension
choices.outcome: You don't fight it. Twenty games and a fine, and it's over -- costly, but survivable, and finally off your back.

choices.label: Deny it, stonewall the investigation
choices.outcome: You fight it on principle and lawyer fees. The investigation grinds on longer than the truth would have, and the league doesn't forget you made them work for it.

events.prompt: Throwing a whole game leaves a different kind of trail than shaving a run, and this time your lawyer isn't talking about games missed. He's talking about the record that follows you the rest of your life.

choices.label: Let the lawyer work it
choices.outcome: He gets the season down to a number instead of a lifetime. It doesn't get you the lifetime part back -- the mark on your name is the one thing he couldn't lawyer away.

events.prompt: The suspension ended months ago. The doors that used to open for you don't anymore -- your name means one thing now, and everybody in the game knows exactly which thing.

choices.label: Take whatever work will have you
choices.outcome: None of it is baseball. All of it pays. You get used to people looking at you like they already know the story.

choices.label: Lean on family instead
choices.outcome: You let them carry some of it with you. It doesn't undo anything, but it's the first night in a while that doesn't feel like carrying it alone.

events.prompt: Your lawyer lays it out plainly: cooperate now, before the league comes looking, and there's a version of this that doesn't end your career. It just ends things with people you don't want ending things with.

choices.label: Cooperate before it catches you
choices.outcome: You get ahead of it -- tell the league everything before they ever come asking. It's the honest way out, and it costs you a set of headlights idling outside your place a few nights later.

text_message.body: Some people came by looking for you. Told them you weren't home.

choices.label: Stay in, it's not worth the risk
choices.outcome: You tell him not yet. Whatever this is, it's not done paying out.

events.prompt: Your lawyer lays it out plainly: cooperate now, before the league comes looking, and there's a version of this that doesn't end your career. It just ends things with people you don't want ending things with.

choices.label: Cooperate before it catches you
choices.outcome: You get ahead of it -- tell the league everything before they ever come asking. It's the honest way out, and it costs you a set of headlights idling outside your place a few nights later.

text_message.body: Some people came by looking for you. Told them you weren't home.

choices.label: Stay in, it's not worth the risk
choices.outcome: You tell him not yet. Whatever this is, it's not done paying out.

events.prompt: You've leaked a line or two, never fixed a result. Coach Reyes doesn't know any of that, but he tells you to be careful who you're spending time with lately, like he half-suspects anyway.

choices.label: Walk away while you still can
choices.outcome: You tell them you're done before you've done anything you can't take back. The marker's still theirs -- but this part, at least, you get to end.

choices.label: Just one more meeting
choices.outcome: You tell yourself you'll walk away after this one. You're still on the hook either way.
